title: The One With the Chicken Pox

expressions.dialogue: Phoebe: Oh ok, Ryan, that guy I **went out with**, who's in the Navy. / Rachel: You **went out with** a guy in the Navy?
expressions.speaker: Phoebe / Rachel
expressions.meaning: 누군가와 연애하다, 사귀다
expressions.nuance: "date someone"보다 캐주얼하고 관계가 확정적이지 않은 느낌. 과거 관계를 회상할 때 자주 씀. 현재도 동일하게 쓰임.
expressions.example: I went out with him for about a year before things got serious. → 진지해지기 전까지 그와 약 1년 정도 사귀었어.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: C'mon, you're going out with the guy! There's gotta be something wrong with him!

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Wait a minute! We're talking about someone that I'm going out with?

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: What's going on here? You go out with tons of girls.

expressions.dialogue: Ross: Yeah, so if you haven't already had it, **chances are** you're gonna get it. / Rachel: Well I've had it.
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.meaning: 아마도, ~할 가능성이 높다
expressions.nuance: "probably"보다 약간 무게감 있지만 일상에서 자주 씀. 데이터나 경험에 근거한 예측임을 암시. 현재도 많이 씀.
expressions.example: Chances are she's already heard the news by now. → 그녀는 아마 이미 소식을 들었을 거야.

occ.speaker: Mrs. Geller
occ.text: Well, at least she had the chance to leave a man at the altar...

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Huh? Are, uh, any of these cultures, per chance, in the tri-state area?

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: No, I know, I'm just nervous. Y'know it's just y'know Mom's dead, don't talk to my sister, Grandma's been sleeping a lot lately. It's like the last desperate chance to have a family, y'know, kinda thing. You're so sweet to wait with me.

expressions.dialogue: Joey: One day you're processing, the next day you're **not so much**... processing any more. / Chandler: I was just telling Joey about the opening in Fleischman's group.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 딱히 그렇지도 않다, 별로 안 그렇다 (완곡한 부정)
expressions.nuance: 직접 부정하는 대신 흐릿하게 부정하는 표현. "not really"보다 건조하고 유머러스한 뉘앙스. 문장 뒤에 단독으로 붙여 쓰는 경우가 많음. 현재도 동일하게 씀.
expressions.example: Do I enjoy early morning meetings? Not so much. → 이른 아침 회의가 좋냐고? 딱히 그렇지도 않아.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Not so much!

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: OK, I have a question. Well, actually, it's not so much a question as.. more of a general wondering... ment.

occ.speaker: David
occ.text: Daryl Hannah was the most beautiful woman that he'd ever seen in his life and I said yeah, I liked her in Splash, a lot, but not so much in- in Wall Street, I thought she had kind of a

expressions.dialogue: Richard: Oh, well that's not so crazy. / Monica: **I'm just easing you in.** / Richard: Oh, alright.
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.meaning: 누군가를 어떤 상황이나 정보에 서서히 익숙하게 하다
expressions.nuance: 충격이나 부담 없이 단계적으로 소개한다는 뉘앙스. "ease into"도 짝을 이루는 표현. 현재도 씀.
expressions.example: Let's ease him into the new system before switching everything over. → 전체 전환 전에 그가 새 시스템에 서서히 적응하게 하자.

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: (singing) "Little, tiny Tarzan, swinging on a nose hair. Swinging with the greatest of ease..." Darn it! Now, I don't know who to get to the next verse.

expressions.dialogue: Monica: If I tell you, you'll think I'm crazy. / Richard: You're pretty much **running that risk** either way.
expressions.speaker: Richard
expressions.meaning: 그 위험을 감수하다, 그 가능성을 안고 가다
expressions.nuance: "take a risk"와 비슷하지만 이미 그 위험 상황 안에 처해 있다는 느낌이 강함. 현재도 씀.
expressions.example: If you skip the backup, you're running the risk of losing everything. → 백업 안 하면 모든 걸 잃을 위험을 감수하는 거야.

expressions.dialogue: Chandler: Hey, look Joey, I'm just saying if you need something to **hold you over**, I can get you a job right here as an entry level processor.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 더 나은 것이 생길 때까지 임시로 버티게 해주다
expressions.nuance: 돈, 음식, 임시 일자리 등에 두루 씀. "tide you over"와 거의 같은 의미. 현재도 씀.
expressions.example: Here's some cash to hold you over until payday. → 월급날까지 버티라고 현금 줄게.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: That's fine. I'll just have a Tic-Tac to hold me over.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Are you sure? (Chandler nods: Yes!) Hold on. (walks over behind the couch) I'm sorry you guys, that was a coffee and a....

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: (opening the door wearing nothing but a sock, and holding a dart board over the `Little General.') Hey! (Chandler turns down the TV) Now, we're not actually gonna be sleeping in her, but do you mind?

expressions.dialogue: Scott: Where do you work? / Joey: Uhh, well, right now I'm **in between things**. You know how it is.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 직업이 없는 상태, 두 일 사이 공백 기간 (실직의 완곡한 표현)
expressions.nuance: "unemployed"를 직접 말하기 불편할 때 쓰는 완곡한 표현. 배우나 프리랜서가 특히 자주 씀. 현재도 동일하게 씀.
expressions.example: She's been in between things since the company downsized. → 회사 구조조정 이후로 그녀는 백수 상태야.

expressions.dialogue: Joey: I just figure Joseph's the kinda guy that likes to **mix it up**. Ya know, get in there, ruffle some feathers. / Chandler: Why?
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 의도적으로 갈등을 일으키다, 판을 흔들다
expressions.nuance: 이 맥락에서는 도발을 즐기는 사람을 묘사함. 스포츠 맥락에서는 몸싸움을 의미하기도 함. 현재도 씀.
expressions.example: He loves to mix it up in debates — always takes the controversial side. → 그는 토론에서 판 흔드는 걸 좋아해 — 항상 논란적인 입장을 취하거든.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Get in there man! Flirt back, mix it up!

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: It's all very, very good. (She covers up and sits down.) So you wanna go uh, mix it up?

expressions.dialogue: Joey: I just figure Joseph's the kinda guy that likes to mix it up. Ya know, get in there, **ruffle some feathers**. / Chandler: Why? / Joey: Look, I'm sorry but that's what Joseph does.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 사람들을 불편하게 하다, 의도적으로 갈등이나 불쾌감을 유발하다
expressions.nuance: 부정적이지만 가끔 "용감하게 문제를 제기한다"는 중립적 뉘앙스로도 씀. 새의 깃털을 거스르는 이미지에서 유래. 현재도 씀.
expressions.example: Her proposal ruffled a few feathers at the board meeting. → 그녀의 제안은 이사회에서 몇 명의 기분을 불편하게 만들었어.

expressions.dialogue: Joey: Mr. Douglas is looking for you. / Chandler: Why? / Joey: 'Cause he has a strong suspicion that you **dropped the ball on the Lender project**.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 중요한 책임을 다하지 못하다, 실수를 저지르다
expressions.nuance: 팀 스포츠에서 공을 놓치는 것에서 유래. 기대됐던 역할을 완수하지 못했을 때 씀. 현재도 매우 자주 씀.
expressions.example: I really dropped the ball on that client presentation. → 그 고객 발표를 완전히 망쳤어.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: It's okay. It's okay. Just pretend that it didn't happen! Okay? No one needs to know! I mean, Phoebe's not an official ballplayer! I mean, only official ballplayers can drop the ball!

occ.speaker: Phoebe-Estelle
occ.text: I guess I dropped the ball there. Whoopsie!

expressions.expression: try to pull something / What are you trying to pull?
expressions.dialogue: Joey: If you **try to pull somethin'**, he'll call you on it. '**What're you tryin' to pull**,' he'll say. / Chandler: Why?
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 속임수를 쓰려 하다, 수상한 짓을 시도하다
expressions.nuance: 상대의 나쁜 의도를 의심하거나 지적할 때 씀. "What are you trying to pull?"은 독립된 관용구로 자주 쓰임. 현재도 씀.
expressions.example: Don't try to pull anything — I know exactly what you're up to. → 수상한 짓 하지 마 — 뭐 하려는지 다 알아.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Somebody wanna help me, tryin' to rip out my heart. (they pull her hand off of him) Uh, that's great. (looking around) Anybody seen a nipple?

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: After she gave me that big speech?! She goes and makes a date with a guy on the same night she has plans with me? I think she's trying to pull a fast one on Big Daddy!

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: 'Cause I know what you're trying to pull here. Okay? It's not gonna work.

expressions.dialogue: Joey: If you try to pull somethin', he'll **call you on it**. 'What're you tryin' to pull,' he'll say. / Chandler: Why?
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 누군가의 잘못된 행동이나 거짓말을 공개적으로 지적하고 따지다
expressions.nuance: 상대가 부정직하거나 부당한 행동을 할 때 당당하게 맞서 지적하는 것. 망설임 없이 직접 문제 삼는 대담한 뉘앙스. 현재도 씀.
expressions.example: She called him on his excuse right in front of everyone. → 그녀는 모두 앞에서 그의 변명을 바로 지적했어.

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Can I borrow the phone? I want to call my apartment and check on my grandma. (to Monica) What's my number?

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Oh, she wants to see me tomorrow...Oh, she sounded really weird, I gotta call Barry... (Does so, on phone) Hi, it's me, I just.. Mindy!! Mindy! Hi! No, I figured that's where you'd be!

occ.speaker: Interviewer
occ.text: And if I want to call for a reference on your last job?

expressions.dialogue: Chandler: It's about **cutting my people a little slack**, ya know, for morale. / Mr. Douglas: I have a family, I'm gonna be here.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 누군가에게 여유를 주다, 엄격하지 않게 너그럽게 봐주다
expressions.nuance: 규칙이나 기대를 잠시 완화해준다는 의미. "Cut me some slack"(나 좀 봐줘) 형태로도 자주 씀. 현재도 아주 많이 씀.
expressions.example: Cut her some slack — it's only her second week. → 좀 봐줘 — 겨우 두 번째 주잖아.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Hey, cut him some slack. It was Chandler's idea.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: (To Ross) Why don't you cut him a little slack? Okay? Maybe if he relaxes a little bit, he'll get some work done.

occ.speaker: Morse
occ.text: Well maybe you can cut me some slack. I'm sort of in love.

expressions.dialogue: Chandler: I hate Joseph, ok. I think he's a **brown-nosing suck-up**. / Joey: Oh yeah. Well you can't fire Joseph.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 상사나 권력자에게 아첨하는 사람, 아부꾼
expressions.nuance: "brown-noser"와 "suck-up" 각각도 독립적으로 씀. "yes-man"보다 더 비하적인 뉘앙스. 약간의 비속어지만 현재도 씀.
expressions.example: Nobody trusts him — he's a total brown-nosing suck-up. → 아무도 그를 믿지 않아 — 완전 아부꾼이거든.

expressions.dialogue: Chandler: Yeah, Chandy, the rogue processor who seduces his co-worker's wives **for sport** and then laughs about it the next day at the water cooler.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 재미 삼아, 스릴을 위해 (진지한 목적이나 이익 없이)
expressions.nuance: 어떤 행동의 목적이 이익이 아닌 순수한 즐거움이나 습관임을 강조. 약간 냉소적이고 부정적인 뉘앙스로 주로 씀. 현재도 씀.
expressions.example: He argues with strangers online just for sport. → 그는 그냥 재미로 온라인에서 모르는 사람들과 싸워.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Let's see. Congress is debating a new deficit reduction bill... the mayor wants to raise subway fares again... the high today was forty-five... and- oh, teams played sports.

expressions.dialogue: Ryan: Phoebe, I have spent the last eight months in a steel tube with men, thinking about this moment. I am not gonna let a bunch of itchy spots **stand between us**.
expressions.speaker: Ryan
expressions.meaning: ~사이를 가로막다, ~을 방해하다
expressions.nuance: 주로 "don't let X stand between you and Y" 형태. 어떤 장애물이 관계나 목표를 방해하게 두지 않겠다는 강한 의지 표현. 현재도 씀.
expressions.example: Don't let a little self-doubt stand between you and your goals. → 약간의 자기 의심이 너와 목표 사이를 가로막게 두지 마.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: No, I'm not questioning it, I'm saying it's stupid! (Notices Monica standing between them and smiling.) What?! (The camera clicks, taking another picture.)

expressions.dialogue: Ross: I'm shipping out tomorrow. / Rachel: Well then uh, we better **make this night count**. [He starts to carry her out.]
expressions.speaker: Rachel
expressions.meaning: ~을 최대한 활용하다, 의미 있게 만들다
expressions.nuance: 시간이나 기회가 제한되어 있을 때 쓰는 표현. "make the most of it"과 유사하지만 더 강렬하고 긴박한 뉘앙스. 현재도 씀.
expressions.example: We only get one shot at this — let's make it count. → 기회는 단 한 번이야 — 제대로 써먹자.

expressions.dialogue: Ross: **I'm shipping out** tomorrow. / Rachel: Well then uh, we better make this night count.
expressions.speaker: Ross (in character)
expressions.meaning: (군인이) 파병되다, 임무를 위해 떠나다
expressions.nuance: 원래 군사 용어이지만 일반적으로 "어딘가로 떠나다"의 의미로도 씀. "shape up or ship out"(고쳐라, 아니면 꺼져라)이라는 관용구로도 유명. 현재도 씀.
expressions.example: Half the unit shipped out to the Middle East last week. → 부대 절반이 지난주에 중동으로 파병됐어.

expressions.dialogue: Rachel: So do you uh, think we can get you one of those uh, uniform things? / Ross: You like that do ya? / Rachel: Oh yeah. / Ross: **I'll make some calls**. [Runs off.]
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.meaning: 내 인맥을 활용해 알아볼게, 연락해볼게
expressions.nuance: 단순히 전화를 한다는 뜻이 아니라 자신의 연줄이나 영향력을 동원하겠다는 뉘앙스. 자신감 있게 일을 처리하겠다는 표현. 현재도 씀.
expressions.example: Need tickets to that sold-out show? Leave it to me — I'll make some calls. → 매진 공연 티켓 필요해? 맡겨봐 — 내가 좀 알아볼게.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: It bit. It was a 50's theme restraunt. I have to cook in a costume and dance on the counter. I mean I was a chef at Cafe des Artistes. I mean how could I take a job where I have to make something called Laverne and Curly Fries?

expressions.dialogue: Phoebe: ...having a picnic at Central Park and ya know, coffee at Central Perk. Oh, **I just got that**. [They kiss.]
expressions.speaker: Phoebe
expressions.meaning: 방금 이해했어, 이제야 알아챘어
expressions.nuance: 말장난이나 숨겨진 의미를 뒤늦게 알아챘을 때 쓰는 표현. "It just hit me"와도 비슷. 현재도 동일하게 씀.
expressions.example: 'Deadline.' Dead. Line. Oh, I just got that. → '데드라인.' 죽다. 선. 아, 이제야 이해했어.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Uh, Yes. Yes. I've just been going over your data here, and little thing, you've been post-dating your Friday numbers.

occ.speaker: Frankie
occ.text: I just got that. Ok, now we'll do your inseam.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Ohh, God, I just got so nervous that he would say no.

expressions.dialogue: Richard: See, if anyone overheard that, I didn't **come off** well. / Monica: Oh my God, you're a freak.
expressions.speaker: Richard
expressions.meaning: (다른 사람 눈에) ~처럼 보이다, ~한 인상을 주다
expressions.nuance: "come off well/badly" 또는 "come off as [형용사]" 형태로 씀. 행동이나 말이 외부에 어떻게 비쳐지는지 표현. 현재도 씀.
expressions.example: He came off as really arrogant in that interview. → 그는 그 인터뷰에서 정말 거만하게 보였어.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: No, it doesn't, does it? But you wanted lumps, Ross? (picks up the pan of badly burnt potatoes) Well, here you go, buddy, ya got one.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: And err, he informed me that uh, she took the news rather badly, in fact, he uh, mentioned the word frenzy.

occ.speaker: Actor
occ.text: (Very melodramatically, and very badly) Oh, that I were a glove upon that hand, that I might... touch thy cheek...

expressions.dialogue: Joey: It's mostly just putting numbers from one column into another column. / Chandler: **Well there you go.**
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 거봐, 그렇지, 됐잖아 (상대의 말이나 상황을 확인·동의해줄 때)
expressions.nuance: 상대가 이미 답을 알고 있거나 상황이 예상대로 됐을 때 씀. 뭔가를 건네줄 때 "자, 여기"의 의미로도 쓰임. 현재도 아주 많이 씀.
expressions.example: 'I figured out the bug on my own.' / 'There you go.' → '혼자 버그 찾아냈어.' / '거봐, 됐잖아.'

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Yeah... yeah, I think there is. -What were you gonna say?

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: There you go.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: He's in the bathroom. I don't think you wanna go in there!

expressions.dialogue: Joey: Joseph and his wife, Karen, are thinking of having a third kid... **Ya know what? Just did.** / Chandler: Really? Wow. That's some pretty powerful imaginary sperm you must have there.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 생각한 김에 바로 그렇게 했어 (즉각적인 결정을 코믹하게 표현)
expressions.nuance: 생각과 행동 사이에 시간이 없음을 과장되게 표현. 이 에피소드에서 Joey와 Chandler가 서로 이 패턴으로 응수함. 현재도 씀.
expressions.example: I was thinking about quitting my gym membership... you know what? Just did. → 헬스장 회원권 끊을까 생각 중이었는데... 그냥 지금 했어.

expressions.dialogue: Chandler: ...if I could just fire Joseph, I would, but unfortunately that's not possible so I'm gonna have to **let both of you go**. / Joey: What're you talking about, everybody loves Joseph.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 해고하다 (완곡한 표현)
expressions.nuance: "fire"의 공식적이고 부드러운 표현. 실제 기업 환경에서 해고 통보 시 광범위하게 씀. 현재도 씀.
expressions.example: Due to budget cuts, we had to let five people go last quarter. → 예산 삭감으로 지난 분기에 직원 다섯 명을 내보내야 했다.

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: If I let go of my hair, my head will fall off.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Thanks! Let's keep going.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Would you let it go? It's not that big a deal.

expressions.dialogue: Mr. Douglas: I have a family, I'm gonna be here. / Joey: Yeah Bing, **what's that about?** / Chandler: It's about cutting my people a little slack.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 그게 무슨 이유야? 왜 그런 거야? (상대의 행동이나 결정에 의문·비판을 제기할 때)
expressions.nuance: 단순한 질문이 아니라 상대방의 행동에 의문이나 불만을 제기하는 뉘앙스. "Why would you do that?"의 구어체 표현. 현재도 씀.
expressions.example: He walked out in the middle of the meeting. What's that about? → 회의 중간에 그냥 나가버렸어. 대체 왜 그런 거야?

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: What is this obsessive need you have to make everyone agree with you? No, what's that all about? I think, I think maybe it's time you put Ross under the microscope.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: (to Phoebe) You know what's weird. Donald Duck never wore pants. But whenever he's getting out of the shower, he always put a towel around his waist. I mean, what is that about?

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: (not seeing Ross) What's your favourite thing about summertime?
